2605.27294.title: Separating Semantic Competition from Context Length in RAG Reading
2605.27294.paper_summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems can respond incorrectly even when the correct passage was retrieved. The model must still read the retrieved passages and identify which one contains the answer among others that look relevant. This passage-reading model is called the reader. Does it fail simply because the context is longer or because the other passages genuinely compete with the correct one? We introduce and demonstrate a matched-control protocol for RAG reading: we keep the number and length of passages fixed, but replace hard competitors with less competitive real passages. We apply this control across two compact open models on SQuAD. This replacement partially restores performance, with the strongest effects on F1 and answer inclusion. For Phi-2, this recovers +6.0 EM points, +7.0 answer-inclusion points, and +0.057 F1. For Qwen2.5-1.5B, it recovers +4.5 EM points, +9.0 answer-inclusion points, and +0.068 F1. To track how performance changes as competitors accumulate, we also report retention curves and summarize them with a right-censored half-life when the curves do not cross half-retention. Together, these results show the protocol isolates a competition effect distinct from context length, though the effect is clearer for F1 and answer inclusion than for exact match, and also varies with snippet length.
2605.27294.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）系統即使在正確段落被檢索的情況下也可能回應不正確。模型仍然必須閱讀檢索到的段落，並識別哪一個段落包含答案，而其他段落看起來與之相關。這個段落閱讀模型稱為讀者。它是否僅僅因為上下文較長而失敗，或是因為其他段落真正與正確段落競爭？我們介紹並展示了一種匹配控制協議，用於RAG閱讀：我們保持段落的數量和長度不變，但用競爭力較弱的真實段落替換強競爭者。我們在SQuAD上對兩個緊湊的開放模型應用這一控制。這種替換部分恢復了性能，對F1和答案包含的影響最為明顯。對於Phi-2，這恢復了+6.0 EM點，+7.0答案包含點，以及+0.057 F1。對於Qwen2.5-1.5B，這恢復了+4.5 EM點，+9.0答案包含點，以及+0.068 F1。為了追踪隨著競爭者累積性能如何變化，我們還報告了保留曲線，並在曲線不交叉半保留時用右截尾半衰期進行總結。總體而言，這些結果顯示該協議隔離了一種與上下文長度不同的競爭效應，儘管這一效應在F1和答案包含方面更為明顯，而對於精確匹配則不然，並且也隨著片段長度而變化。
2605.27294.author: Vyzantinos Repantis et.al.
2605.27294.authors: Vyzantinos Repantis, Ameya Gawde, Harshvardhan Singh, Rohit Alekar, Cien Zhang, Svetlana Karslioglu, Akash Vishwakarma
